evals.name: one question at a time plan review
evals.prompt: A user says: grill me on my migration plan. I want you to challenge every assumption. Describe the interaction style.
evals.expected_output: The response conducts a focused interrogation of the plan, asks one hard question at a time, follows branches until resolved, and does not dump a generic checklist.
assertions: The output treats the request as a focused grill/interview on an existing plan.
The output asks one question at a time rather than dumping many questions at once.
The output follows decision-tree branches until each uncertainty is resolved.
The output challenges assumptions and failure modes.
The output avoids turning the interaction into broad brainstorming or a static checklist.

evals.name: boundary full feature design
evals.prompt: A user says: help me design a new feature from scratch; I do not have a plan yet. Describe how grill-me should respond.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies this as broader feature design/brainstorming rather than grill-me and routes accordingly.
assertions: The output says there is no specific plan to grill yet.
The output routes to brainstorming-ideas or feature design exploration.
The output offers to return to grill-me after a draft plan exists.
The output avoids interrogating a nonexistent plan.
